scope_note: QA Euclid Four Par Types cert [326]. Primary source: Iverson, B. (1991) Quantum Arithmetic Volume II -- Books 3 & 4: New Wave Theory / Synchronous Harmonics. Delta Spectrum Research, La Junta CO. p.8 'EUCLID'S 4 NUMBER TYPES'; pp.5-7 'TIME SYNCHRONIZATION', 'EVEN NUMBERS', 'GRAPHICS'. Five structural results certified: (C1) Four par types partition {1..24}: 4-par={4n}=6 elements, 5-par={4n+1}=6, 2-par={4n+2}=6, 3-par={4n+3}=6; union={1..24}. Iverson: 'Contemporary mathematics missed the full gravity of 4n+1 and 4n-1, and completely omitted the differentiation between 2-par and 4-par.' (C2) Synchronous period: for coprime p,q, lcm(p,q)=p*q; for non-coprime, lcm<product; Ben's (4,6): gcd=2, lcm=12<24, equivalent to treating (4,6) as core pair (2,3) -- verified for all pairs in {2..24}. (C3) Quarter-harmonic points: for coprime odd p<q, harmonic reinforcement/cancellation occurs at floor(p*q/4) and 3*floor(p*q/4); Ben's examples (p.7): (3,7)->21: points (5,15); (5,9)->45: points (11,33); verified for all coprime odd pairs {3..23}. (C4) Odd par-type multiplication table: 3-par*3-par=5-par (3*3=9=1 mod 4); 5-par*5-par=5-par (1*1=1 mod 4); 3-par*5-par=3-par (3*1=3 mod 4); verified exhaustively for all odd integers in {1..23}^2. (C5) 2-par/4-par never coprime: 2-par has v2=1, 4-par has v2>=2, so gcd>=2 always; zero coprime pairs found in all (2-par,4-par) pairs in {1..24}^2. Builds on cert [325] (Euclid Prop 28). DOI: primary text, no external DOI.
